Class Page

java.lang.Object
io.github.sentinel.system.YAMLObject
io.github.sentinel.pages.Page
public class Page extends YAMLObject
Page class to contain the details of an page.

  • Constructor Details

    • Page

      public Page(String pageName)
      Constructor
      Parameters:
      pageName - String the exact name of the page as stored on disk without extension.

  • Method Details

    • getElement

      public Element getElement(String elementName)
      Returns an Element if it exists in the page object yaml file. If the element name contains spaces they are replaced by underscores, and all characters are made lowercase. The element is created if it has not been loaded yet, but otherwise is pulled from memory. In this way we only dynamically load the elements used and do not try to process an entire page object file at once.

      Note that Element object itself handles finding the element on the page and that is always handled dynamically.

      Parameters:
      elementName - String the name of the element to find
      Returns:
      io.github.sentinel.Element the Element object found

    • clearTables

      public void clearTables()
      Clears the cached Table objects for this page. This action prevents StaleElementReferenceException when a table is referenced after previous navigation.

    • hasIFrames

      public boolean hasIFrames()
      Returns true if iFrames exist on the page, false if they do not.
      Returns:
      true if iFrames exist on the page, false if they do not

    • getIFrames

      public List<org.openqa.selenium.WebElement> getIFrames()
      Returns a list of WebElement objects containing all the iFrames on the page.
      Returns:
      List <WebElement> the list of iFrames in this page

    • getPageObjectType

      public PageObjectType getPageObjectType()
      Returns either WEBPAGE or EXECUTABLE based on the type of page object this is.
      Returns:
      PageObjectType the type of page object (WEBPAGE, EXECUTABLE, UNKNOWN)

    • isJsAlertPresent

      public boolean isJsAlertPresent()
      Returns true if a Javascript alert is present. False if an alert is not found. Driver returns back to the previous window's context if the alert is found.
      Returns:
      boolean true if an alert is present, false otherwise.

    • getJsAlertText

      public String getJsAlertText()
      Gets the text on the JS alert.
      Returns:
      String the text in the JS alert
